LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANTS WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A., WELLS FARGO & COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. PARAGRAPH 12 OF THE CROSS-COMPLAINT INCORPORATES THE COMPLAINT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A., WELLS FARGO & COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TOTHE COMMON LAW CONVERSION AND UCC 3420 CAUSES OF ACTION. THE DEMURRER TO THE NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. THE NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION INCORPORATES NUMEROUS INSTANCES OF NEGLIGENCE AND DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW THAT THE ENTIRE CAUSE OF ACTION INS PREEMPTED. THE DEMURRER TO THE NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS IS NOT A DISTINCT CAUSE OF ACTION AND EMOTIONAL DISTRESS DAMAGES ARE NOT RECOVERABLE IN THIS CASE. SEE BRO V. GLASER (1994) 22 CAL.APP.4TH 1398. HOWEVER, AT THE HEARING ON DEMURRER, PLAINTIFFS' COUNSEL STATED THAT PLAINTIFFS DON'T INTEND TO AMEND THESE CAUSES OF ACTION. ACCORDINGLY, THESE CLAIMS ARE DISMISSED. DEFENDANTS HAVE 15 DAYS FROM NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER TO ANSWER PLAINITFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

Off Calendar Mar-25-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: geoffrey evers, esq., 916.974.3000, for plaintiff rosario oconnor.
jonah van zandt, esq., 415.677.5568, for defendant wells fargo bank, n.a.
elizabeth frye, esq., 213.689.8880, for defendant east west bank.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTs WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A., WELLS FARGO & COMPANY's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as tothe common law conversion and UCC 3420 causes of action. The demurrer to the negligence cause of action is overruled. The negligence cause of action incorporates numerous instances of negligence and Defendant fails to show that the entire cause of action ins preempted. The demurrer to the negligent infliction of emotional distress cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. Negligent infliction of emotional distress is not a distinct cause of action and emotional distress damages are not recoverable in this case. See Bro v. Glaser (1994) 22 Cal.App.4th 1398. However, at the hearing on demurrer, plaintiffs' counsel stated that plaintiffs don't intend to amend these causes of action. accordingly, these claims are dismissed. defendants have 15 days from notice of entry of order to answer plainitff's 1st amended complaint. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

*** AFTER TAKING MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION, The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant East West Bank's Motion To Change Venue From San Francisco County To Los Angeles is denied. Venue is proper because Defendant Wells Fargo Bank's principal place of business is in San Francisco County. Defendant East West Bank has failed in meeting its burden regarding the convenience of witnesses. The convenience of party witnesses is generally not material to a motion brought to change venue on the basis of forum non conveniens, and moving party does not explain how the convenience of the few non-party witnesses would be affected by maintaining the case in San Francisco. ORDER SIGNED. ***
